channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Unsightly and growing area ofç fly tipping, 23rd April
item.description:
For weeks now nobody is cleaning up the fly tipping by the communal bin area. It is piling higher and higher (see photos that evidence this). It looks terrible and is a fire risk.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jamaica Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic jam and honking, 23rd April
item.description:
The area where I live is being strangled by a constant traffic jam along the embankment towards Battersea bridge, and all the entries to the bridge along Kings Road, Beaufort street, Old Church Street and recently Danvers Street. The honking coming from the Embankment has become a permanent feature any day and time of the week. The level of noise and air pollution is terrible and the quality of life in this neighbourhood is declining drastically. All because the council is not managing the overflow of traffic into the local bridges. The neighbourhood is being destroyed by the commuters. Can we please protect the residents from having their streets invaded by relentless traffic? It’s 10:30pm and the traffic jam along the embankment is still ongoing. I would like to go to sleep but the drivers are furious and there is a lot of honking. There is no peace even at night. Since the number of bridges in this city is decreasing while the number of cars is increasing, I only see one way to resolve this situation - extend the congestion charge and introduce bans to nonlocals on driving through certain streets that are used as short cuts (like Danvers Street) during rush hour,. Some councils already do that. This council needs to do more to protect its residents well being!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheyne Walk, Chelsea, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street street Cleaning and flytipping, 23rd April
item.description:
1) I walk daily to the train station, it is depressing walking down the road, litter everywhere in the tree stumps too. Road is not being swept weekly. 2) Flytipping midway in Willenhall Rd resident placing boxes into a clear sack but it is thrown around the street. Lack of education to HMO properties. Why are these properties allowed to leave builders waste and other household outside their homes. No Enforcement being carried out on these properties. Pay council tax and the place is dirty every week. Please can the properties on Brookhill Rd be visited and the landlords contacted concerning constant waste out on the street.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brumwell Avenue, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish piling up on unswept public pavement, 23rd April
item.description:
This pavement area has not been swept for weeks on end. Rubbish lying about, area is unkempt. Please see photos.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Approx 60 boxes of dried pasta, ply board, door hinges and glass dumped in the road causing a hazard, 23rd April
item.description:
Van with tail lift seen at the entrance of the close off loaded this commercial waste. Did not see this happening. CCTV possibly on Pure Lake site may have CCTV footage. Vehicle possibly took money from a shop then dumped the waste. Brookhill Close junction with Brookhill Rd opposite no 19 Brookhill Close SE18 6UD. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookhill Close, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loose wooden board on children's playground, 23rd April
item.description:
Board needs screws to get fixed to the existing bridge. Potential risk of children stepping on the gap if the board gets displaced.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Patina Walk, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 36 Lordship Lane (in front) Flytipping, 23rd April
item.description:
Furniture and other rubbish dumped. Needs removal as it is a hazard for pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 57, Ashbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London

item.description:
Broken Glass on the pavement needs to be cleared.

item.title: Van stationery for over 2 months, 23rd April
item.description:
Van is there and hasn't moved in months. Needs to be moved on.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Villiers Road, Penge, Greater London

item.title: Potholes causing buildings to shake, 23rd April
item.description:
Pot holes are reemerging. When buses go over them nearby buildings shake.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 71, Lawrie Park Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.title: No lighting again whole.street, 23rd April
item.description:
Again the lights are oit this is a weekly thing now. The whole of. Hill View Drive se28 0lj is out possible corners of.High tor and teasel cresent

item.title: Open electricity / telecommunications cabinet, 23rd April

item.title: Graffiti on Golders Green Hippodrome, 23rd April
item.description:
Graffiti has significantly increased on the Golders Green Hippodrome near the bus stop and railway yards.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped on pavement, 23rd April
